title: Fix screenshot analysis: simplify Gemini prompt for basic next-candle prediction

requirements.text: Rewrite the Gemini API prompt in frontend/src/services/analysisApi.ts to be a minimal, direct instruction focused solely on predicting the next candle direction. The new prompt must: (1) tell Gemini to look at the image and find the candlestick chart area, ignoring everything else on the screen; (2) examine the last 5 visible candles and the overall trend; (3) predict whether the NEXT candle is more likely to be bullish or bearish; (4) return ONLY a valid JSON object with these fields: sinal ('COMPRA' if next candle likely bullish, 'VENDA' if likely bearish, 'SEM ENTRADA' if unclear), tendencia ('ALTA'/'BAIXA'/'LATERAL'), confianca (number 0-100), padroes (array of short Portuguese pattern name strings, max 3 items), explicacao (one sentence in Portuguese explaining the prediction). The prompt must be short (under 200 words), use plain language, and explicitly state that Gemini must respond with raw JSON only — no markdown, no code blocks, no extra text.

quotes: nao esta analisando print, tenda fazer analise basica, para proxima vela, sem mudar interface do aplicativo

acceptanceCriteria: The prompt text in analysisApi.ts is under 200 words
The prompt explicitly requests a JSON-only response with no markdown or code fences
The prompt asks Gemini to identify the candlestick chart area within the full screenshot
The prompt asks for next-candle direction prediction based on the last 5 candles
The prompt requests exactly these fields: sinal, tendencia, confianca, padroes, explicacao
No complex multi-step scoring system or broker-specific detection logic is present in the prompt

requirements.text: Update the Gemini API response parsing logic in frontend/src/services/analysisApi.ts to robustly extract JSON from the Gemini response text. Gemini sometimes wraps JSON in markdown code fences (```json ... ```) or adds surrounding text. The parser must: strip any markdown code fence wrappers before JSON.parse; attempt to extract the first {...} block from the response text if direct parsing fails; log the raw response text to the console for debugging; throw a descriptive AnalysisApiError with Portuguese message 'Resposta inválida da IA — tente novamente' if no valid JSON can be extracted.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser strips markdown code fences (```json and ```) before attempting JSON.parse
Parser falls back to extracting the first { } block if direct parse fails
Raw Gemini response text is logged to the browser console on every request
An AnalysisApiError with Portuguese message is thrown when JSON cannot be extracted
Valid JSON responses parse correctly without modification

requirements.text: Update the image-to-base64 conversion step in frontend/src/services/analysisApi.ts to ensure the image is always sent as a clean base64 string. Remove the base64 data URI prefix (e.g., 'data:image/jpeg;base64,') before placing the value in the inlineData.data field — Gemini Vision API requires raw base64 data without the data URI prefix. Also ensure the mimeType field is correctly set to the actual file MIME type (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'image/png', 'image/webp') and never hardcoded to a fixed value.
acceptanceCriteria: The data URI prefix 'data:[mimetype];base64,' is stripped before placing base64 data in inlineData.data
The mimeType value reflects the actual uploaded file's type
The base64 string sent to Gemini is raw (no prefix)
A console.log confirms the mimeType and base64 length before the fetch call

constraints: Do not change any UI components, page layouts, or visual design
Do not alter any immutable frontend paths
Changes are limited to frontend/src/services/analysisApi.ts only
nonGoals: Changing the app interface or visual design in any way
Adding new UI sections or result fields
Modifying backend data models or storage
Changing the Settings, Results, History, or ProcessingScreen pages
